verses.latin_vulgate: Et conveniunt ad eum pharisæi, et quidam de scribis, venientes ab Jerosolymis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ συνάγονται πρὸς αὐτὸν οἱ φαρισαῖοι καί τινες τῶν γραμματέων ἐλθόντες ἀπὸ ἱεροσολύμων
verses.king_james: Then came together unto him the Pharisees, and certain of the scribes, which came from Jerusalem.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum vidissent quosdam ex discipulis ejus communibus manibus, id est non lotis, manducare panes, vituperaverunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἰδόντες τινὰς τῶν μαθητῶν αὐτοῦ κοιναῖς χερσίν τοῦτ᾽ ἔστιν ἀνίπτοις ἐσθίοντας ἄρτους ἐμέμψαντο
verses.king_james: And when they saw some of his disciples eat bread with defiled, that is to say, with unwashen, hands, they found fault.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pharisæi enim, et omnes Judæi, nisi crebro laverint manus, non manducant, tenentes traditionem seniorum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ γὰρ φαρισαῖοι καὶ πάντες οἱ ἰουδαῖοι ἐὰν μὴ πυγμῇ νίψωνται τὰς χεῖρας οὐκ ἐσθίουσιν κρατοῦντες τὴν παράδοσιν τῶν πρεσβυτέρων
verses.king_james: For the Pharisees, and all the Jews, except they wash *their* hands oft, eat not, holding the tradition of the elders.

verses.latin_vulgate: et a foro nisi baptizentur, non comedunt: et alia multa sunt, quæ tradita sunt illis servare, baptismata calicum, et urceorum, et æramentorum, et lectorum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀπό ἀγορᾶς ἐὰν μὴ βαπτίσωνται οὐκ ἐσθίουσιν καὶ ἄλλα πολλά ἐστιν ἃ παρέλαβον κρατεῖν βαπτισμοὺς ποτηρίων καὶ ξεστῶν καὶ χαλκίων καὶ κλινῶν
verses.king_james: And *when they come* from the market, except they wash, they eat not. And many other things there be, which they have received to hold, *as* the washing of cups, and pots, brasen vessels, and of tables.

verses.latin_vulgate: et interrogabant eum pharisæi et scribæ: Quare discipuli tui non ambulant juxta traditionem seniorum, sed communibus manibus manducant panem?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔπειτα ἐπερωτῶσιν αὐτὸν οἱ φαρισαῖοι καὶ οἱ γραμματεῖς διὰτι οἱ μαθηταί σου οὐ περιπατοῦσιν κατὰ τὴν παράδοσιν τῶν πρεσβυτέρων ἀλλὰ ἀνίπτοις χερσὶν ἐσθίουσιν τὸν ἄρτον
verses.king_james: Then the Pharisees and scribes asked him, Why walk not thy disciples according to the tradition of the elders, but eat bread with unwashen hands?

verses.latin_vulgate: At ille respondens, dixit eis: Bene prophetavit Isaias de vobis hypocritis, sicut scriptum est: [Populus hic labiis me honorat, cor autem eorum longe est a me:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἀποκριθεὶς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς ὅτι καλῶς προεφήτευσεν ἠσαΐας περὶ ὑμῶν τῶν ὑποκριτῶν ὡς γέγραπται οὗτος ὁ λαὸς τοῖς χείλεσίν με τιμᾷ ἡ δὲ καρδία αὐτῶν πόῤῥω ἀπέχει ἀπ᾽ ἐμοῦ
verses.king_james: He answered and said unto them, Well hath Esaias prophesied of you hypocrites, as it is written, This people honoureth me with *their* lips, but their heart is far from me.

verses.latin_vulgate: in vanum autem me colunt, docentes doctrinas, et præcepta hominum.]
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μάτην δὲ σέβονταί με διδάσκοντες διδασκαλίας ἐντάλματα ἀνθρώπων
verses.king_james: Howbeit in vain do they worship me, teaching *for* doctrines the commandments of men.

verses.latin_vulgate: Relinquentes enim mandatum Dei, tenetis traditionem hominum, baptismata urceorum et calicum: et alia similia his facitis multa.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀφέντες γὰρ τὴν ἐντολὴν τοῦ θεοῦ κρατεῖτε τὴν παράδοσιν τῶν ἀνθρώπων βαπτισμοὺς ξεστῶν καὶ ποτηρίων καὶ ἀλλὰ παρόμοια τοιαῦτα πολλὰ ποιεῖτε
verses.king_james: For laying aside the commandment of God, ye hold the tradition of men, *as* the washing of pots and cups: and many other such like things ye do.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dicebat illis: Bene irritum facitis præceptum Dei, ut traditionem vestram servetis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἔλεγεν αὐτοῖς καλῶς ἀθετεῖτε τὴν ἐντολὴν τοῦ θεοῦ ἵνα τὴν παράδοσιν ὑμῶν τηρήσητε
verses.king_james: And he said unto them, Full well ye reject the commandment of God, that ye may keep your own tradition.

verses.latin_vulgate: Moyses enim dixit: Honora patrem tuum, et matrem tuam. Et: Qui maledixerit patri, vel matri, morte moriatur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μωσῆς γὰρ εἶπεν τίμα τὸν πατέρα σου καὶ τὴν μητέρα σου καί ὁ κακολογῶν πατέρα ἢ μητέρα θανάτῳ τελευτάτω
verses.king_james: For Moses said, Honour thy father and thy mother; and, Whoso curseth father or mother, let him die the death:

verses.latin_vulgate: Vos autem dicitis: Si dixerit homo patri, aut matri, Corban (quod est donum) quodcumque ex me, tibi profuerit:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑμεῖς δὲ λέγετε ἐὰν εἴπῃ ἄνθρωπος τῷ πατρὶ ἢ τῇ μητρί κορβᾶν ὅ ἐστιν δῶρον ὃ ἐὰν ἐξ ἐμοῦ ὠφεληθῇς
verses.king_james: But ye say, If a man shall say to his father or mother, *It is* Corban, that is to say, a gift, by whatsoever thou mightest be profited by me; *he shall be free*.

verses.latin_vulgate: et ultra non dimittitis eum quidquam facere patri suo, aut matri,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οὐκέτι ἀφίετε αὐτὸν οὐδὲν ποιῆσαι τῷ πατρὶ αὐτοῦ ἢ τῇ μητρί αὐτοῦ,
verses.king_james: And ye suffer him no more to do ought for his father or his mother;

verses.latin_vulgate: rescindentes verbum Dei per traditionem vestram, quam tradidistis: et similia hujusmodi multa facitis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀκυροῦντες τὸν λόγον τοῦ θεοῦ τῇ παραδόσει ὑμῶν ᾗ παρεδώκατε καὶ παρόμοια τοιαῦτα πολλὰ ποιεῖτε
verses.king_james: Making the word of God of none effect through your tradition, which ye have delivered: and many such like things do ye.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et advocans iterum turbam, dicebat illis: Audite me omnes, et intelligite.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ προσκαλεσάμενος πάντα τὸν ὄχλον ἔλεγεν αὐτοῖς ἀκούετέ μου πάντες καὶ συνίετε
verses.king_james: And when he had called all the people *unto him*, he said unto them, Hearken unto me every one *of you*, and understand:

verses.latin_vulgate: Nihil est extra hominem introiens in eum, quod possit eum coinquinare, sed quæ de homine procedunt illa sunt quæ communicant hominem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐδέν ἐστιν ἔξωθεν τοῦ ἀνθρώπου εἰσπορευόμενον εἰς αὐτὸν ὃ δύναται αὐτόν κοινῶσαι ἀλλὰ τὰ ἐκπορευόμενά ἀπ᾽ αὐτοῦ, ἐκεῖνά ἐστιν τὰ κοινοῦντα τὸν ἄνθρωπον
verses.king_james: There is nothing from without a man, that entering into him can defile him: but the things which come out of him, those are they that defile the man.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si quis habet aures audiendi, audiat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἴ τις ἔχει ὦτα ἀκούειν, ἀκουέτω
verses.king_james: If any man have ears to hear, let him hear.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum introisset in domum a turba, interrogabant eum discipuli ejus parabolam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὅτε εἰσῆλθεν εἰς οἶκον ἀπὸ τοῦ ὄχλου ἐπηρώτων αὐτὸν οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ περὶ τὴς παραβολής
verses.king_james: And when he was entered into the house from the people, his disciples asked him concerning the parable.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait illis: Sic et vos imprudentes estis? Non intelligitis quia omne extrinsecus introiens in hominem, non potest eum communicare:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ λέγει αὐτοῖς οὕτως καὶ ὑμεῖς ἀσύνετοί ἐστε οὐ νοεῖτε ὅτι πᾶν τὸ ἔξωθεν εἰσπορευόμενον εἰς τὸν ἄνθρωπον οὐ δύναται αὐτὸν κοινῶσαι
verses.king_james: And he saith unto them, Are ye so without understanding also? Do ye not perceive, that whatsoever thing from without entereth into the man, *it* cannot defile him;

verses.latin_vulgate: quia non intrat in cor ejus, sed in ventrum vadit, et in secessum exit, purgans omnes escas?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι οὐκ εἰσπορεύεται αὐτοῦ εἰς τὴν καρδίαν ἀλλ᾽ εἰς τὴν κοιλίαν καὶ εἰς τὸν ἀφεδρῶνα ἐκπορεύεται καθαρίζον πάντα τὰ βρώματα
verses.king_james: Because it entereth not into his heart, but into the belly, and goeth out into the draught, purging all meats?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicebat autem, quoniam quæ de homine exeunt, illa communicant hominem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔλεγεν δὲ ὅτι τὸ ἐκ τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ἐκπορευόμενον ἐκεῖνο κοινοῖ τὸν ἄνθρωπον
verses.king_james: And he said, That which cometh out of the man, that defileth the man.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ab intus enim de corde hominum malæ cogitationes procedunt, adulteria, fornicationes, homicidia,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔσωθεν γὰρ ἐκ τῆς καρδίας τῶν ἀνθρώπων οἱ διαλογισμοὶ οἱ κακοὶ ἐκπορεύονται μοιχεῖαι, πορνεῖαι φόνοι
verses.king_james: For from within, out of the heart of men, proceed evil thoughts, adulteries, fornications, murders,

verses.latin_vulgate: furta, avaritiæ, nequitiæ, dolus, impudicitiæ, oculus malus, blasphemia, superbia, stultitia.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κλοπαί πλεονεξίαι πονηρίαι δόλος ἀσέλγεια ὀφθαλμὸς πονηρός βλασφημία ὑπερηφανία ἀφροσύνη
verses.king_james: Thefts, covetousness, wickedness, deceit, lasciviousness, an evil eye, blasphemy, pride, foolishness:

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnia hæc mala ab intus procedunt, et communicant hominem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πάντα ταῦτα τὰ πονηρὰ ἔσωθεν ἐκπορεύεται καὶ κοινοῖ τὸν ἄνθρωπον
verses.king_james: All these evil things come from within, and defile the man.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et inde surgens abiit in fines Tyri et Sidonis: et ingressus domum, neminem voluit scire, et non potuit latere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐκεῖθεν ἀναστὰς ἀπῆλθεν εἰς τὰ μεθόρια τύρου καὶ σιδῶνος καὶ εἰσελθὼν εἰς τὴν οἰκίαν οὐδένα ἤθελεν γνῶναι καὶ οὐκ ἠδυνήθη λαθεῖν
verses.king_james: And from thence he arose, and went into the borders of Tyre and Sidon, and entered into an house, and would have no man know *it*: but he could not be hid.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mulier enim statim ut audivit de eo, cujus filia habebat spiritum immundum, intravit, et procidit ad pedes ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀκούσασα γὰρ γυνὴ περὶ αὐτοῦ ἡς εἶχεν τὸ θυγάτριον αὐτῆς πνεῦμα ἀκάθαρτον ἐλθοῦσα προσέπεσεν πρὸς τοὺς πόδας αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: For a *certain* woman, whose young daughter had an unclean spirit, heard of him, and came and fell at his feet:

verses.latin_vulgate: Erat enim mulier gentilis, Syrophœnissa genere. Et rogabat eum ut dæmonium ejiceret de filia ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦν δὲ ἡ γυνὴ ἑλληνίς συροφοινίσσα τῷ γένει καὶ ἠρώτα αὐτὸν ἵνα τὸ δαιμόνιον ἐκβάλλῃ ἐκ τῆς θυγατρὸς αὐτῆς
verses.king_james: The woman was a Greek, a Syrophenician by nation; and she besought him that he would cast forth the devil out of her daughter.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui dixit illi: Sine prius saturari filios: non est enim bonum sumere panem filiorum, et mittere canibus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἰησοῦς εἶπεν αὐτῇ ἄφες πρῶτον χορτασθῆναι τὰ τέκνα οὐ γάρ καλὸν ἐστιν λαβεῖν τὸν ἄρτον τῶν τέκνων καὶ βαλεῖν τοῖς κυναρίοις
verses.king_james: But Jesus said unto her, Let the children first be filled: for it is not meet to take the children's bread, and to cast *it* unto the dogs.

verses.latin_vulgate: At illa respondit, et dixit illi: Utique Domine, nam et catelli comedunt sub mensa de micis puerorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ δὲ ἀπεκρίθη καὶ λέγει αὐτῷ ναὶ, κύριε καὶ γὰρ τὰ κυνάρια ὑποκάτω τῆς τραπέζης ἐσθίει ἀπὸ τῶν ψιχίων τῶν παιδίων
verses.king_james: And she answered and said unto him, Yes, Lord: yet the dogs under the table eat of the children's crumbs.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait illi: Propter hunc sermonem vade: exiit dæmonium a filia tua.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶπεν αὐτῇ διὰ τοῦτον τὸν λόγον ὕπαγε ἐξελήλυθεν τὸ δαιμόνιον ἐκ τῆς θυγατρός σου
verses.king_james: And he said unto her, For this saying go thy way; the devil is gone out of thy daughter.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum abiisset domum suam, invenit puellam jacentem supra lectum, et dæmonium exiisse.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀπελθοῦσα εἰς τὸν οἶκον αὐτῆς εὗρεν τὸ δαιμόνιον ἐξεληλυθός καὶ τὴν θυγατερα βεβλημένην ἐπὶ τὴς κλίνης
verses.king_james: And when she was come to her house, she found the devil gone out, and her daughter laid upon the bed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et iterum exiens de finibus Tyri, venit per Sidonem ad mare Galilææ inter medios fines Decapoleos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πάλιν ἐξελθὼν ἐκ τῶν ὁρίων τύρου καὶ σιδῶνος ἦλθεν πρὸς τὴν θάλασσαν τῆς γαλιλαίας ἀνὰ μέσον τῶν ὁρίων δεκαπόλεως
verses.king_james: And again, departing from the coasts of Tyre and Sidon, he came unto the sea of Galilee, through the midst of the coasts of Decapolis.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et adducunt ei surdum, et mutum, et deprecabantur eum, ut imponat illi manum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ φέρουσιν αὐτῷ κωφὸν μογιλάλον καὶ παρακαλοῦσιν αὐτὸν ἵνα ἐπιθῇ αὐτῷ τὴν χεῖρα
verses.king_james: And they bring unto him one that was deaf, and had an impediment in his speech; and they beseech him to put his hand upon him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et apprehendens eum de turba seorsum, misit digitos suos in auriculas ejus: et exspuens, tetigit linguam ejus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀπολαβόμενος αὐτὸν ἀπὸ τοῦ ὄχλου κατ᾽ ἰδίαν ἔβαλεν τοὺς δακτύλους αὐτοῦ εἰς τὰ ὦτα αὐτοῦ καὶ πτύσας ἥψατο τῆς γλώσσης αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And he took him aside from the multitude, and put his fingers into his ears, and he spit, and touched his tongue;

verses.latin_vulgate: et suscipiens in cælum, ingemuit, et ait illi: Ephphetha, quod est, Adaperire.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀναβλέψας εἰς τὸν οὐρανὸν ἐστέναξεν καὶ λέγει αὐτῷ εφφαθα ὅ ἐστιν διανοίχθητι
verses.king_james: And looking up to heaven, he sighed, and saith unto him, Ephphatha, that is, Be opened.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et statim apertæ sunt aures ejus, et solutum est vinculum linguæ ejus, et loquebatur recte.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εὐθέως διηνοίχθησαν αὐτοῦ αἱ ἀκοαί καὶ ἐλύθη ὁ δεσμὸς τῆς γλώσσης αὐτοῦ καὶ ἐλάλει ὀρθῶς
verses.king_james: And straightway his ears were opened, and the string of his tongue was loosed, and he spake plain.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et præcepit illis ne cui dicerent. Quanto autem eis præcipiebat, tanto magis plus prædicabant:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ διεστείλατο αὐτοῖς ἵνα μηδενὶ εἴπωσιν ὅσον δὲ αὐτὸς αὐτοῖς διεστέλλετο μᾶλλον περισσότερον ἐκήρυσσον
verses.king_james: And he charged them that they should tell no man: but the more he charged them, so much the more a great deal they published *it*;

verses.latin_vulgate: et eo amplius admirabantur, dicentes: Bene omnia fecit: et surdos fecit audire, et mutos loqui.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὑπερπερισσῶς ἐξεπλήσσοντο λέγοντες καλῶς πάντα πεποίηκεν καὶ τοὺς κωφοὺς ποιεῖ ἀκούειν καὶ τοὺς ἀλάλους λαλεῖν
verses.king_james: And were beyond measure astonished, saying, He hath done all things well: he maketh both the deaf to hear, and the dumb to speak.
